title: Combat Freeze Hotfix
entries: Fixed a crash that could freeze the game view after attacking or drawing enemy aggro, while movement and pet name labels could still appear to move.
This hotfix restores the missing animal aggro guard from the cleanup pass without changing combat balance.

title: Meteor Timer Mining and XP Potion Guard
entries: Meteor mining now settles from the server timer instead of client-side hit messages, so hidden tabs, mobile sleep, reconnects, and walking away right after returning can still claim the swings you already earned.
Meteor catch-up after a slept or backgrounded tab is less capped by old local state and now claims owed mainland and island meteor ticks through the relay.
Experience Potions now have a short 1.5-second sip guard after a successful drink, with the inventory or quick slot showing a light clockwise dark overlay while the next sip is blocked.
The Settings tester credits now include sugi and nao-ki with niwa and yuma.

title: Cleanup, Exchange Polish and Guest Drop Safety
entries: Gathered resources now use one consistent stackability policy, and the old Magic Bag upgrade has been removed from live item lists. Old Magic Bag entries are cleaned from saves.
Fresh total level displays now use the live skill list, so new HUD, admin, and remote player totals start at the correct value.
Guest sessions can no longer drop items for other players, claim player drops, or publish local ground drops to multiplayer.
Icebergs are safer to walk on again. When you step onto a floe, the surface wins over the water underneath instead of leaving you stuck inside it.
Exchange and store item hovers now use the proper equipment tooltip, with cleaner sell-offer search sizing and slimmer scrollbars.
The Hologram slime icon now matches between the SlimeCoin Shop preview and minimap/player icon, with darker face details.
Washer SlimePower numbers now sit in the bottom-left of item tiles with a readable color dot and number.
Tutorial markers for Prayer, Compost, Scribe, and Task Board now point at the live UI and world services.

title: Meteor Catch-Up and Register Fixes
entries: Meteor catch-up after a hidden, locked, or mobile-backgrounded tab is more exact. If you were already mining an Island Meteor, owed swings now settle through the server even if you walk away right after returning.
Meteor wake-up rewards are quieter on return, so catch-up creates less separate XP and drop text before the final settlement.
The Register button inside Settings now uses the same account creation path as the start screen, so guest progress, account scope, session lock, and save transfer behave consistently.
Remembered accounts get a short extra session check before being asked to log in, reducing false login prompts on slow mobile tab resumes.
New characters now keep Hitpoints level 10 correctly on first progress sync.

title: Shop, Trade and Meteor Polish
entries: The Hologram slime skin now previews correctly in the SlimeCoin Shop and uses a clearer hologram-style icon.
The SlimeCoin Shop layout is steadier on mobile, with names, prices, previews, and buy buttons staying aligned.
Trading now requires both players to be signed in, and guest accounts no longer show or accept trade actions.
Trade accept and completion feedback now appears as a centered result toast instead of a full-screen green flash.
Meteor rewards that settle after returning from a hidden or mobile backgrounded tab now combine into one compact world popup instead of a wall of stacked text.
The duplicate-tab screen now recovers sooner when an old phone tab has actually stopped responding, while still keeping Test Mode explicit.

title: Registered Name Fix
entries: Guest play now checks more than the first few matching leaderboard rows when a name belongs to a registered account, so older guest rows no longer hide the real account owner.
Registered names with older duplicate guest rows, such as accounts that played before the guest leaderboard write lock, are now reserved correctly.

title: Account Session Safety
entries: If your browser remembers a logged-in account but the session is gone, Play now pauses and asks you to log back in instead of starting a fresh guest character.
Guest play now refuses a confirmed registered account name and asks you to log in to use it.

title: AFK Gather and UI Polish
entries: Returning from a hidden, slept, or closed tab now settles active gathering immediately, including meteor and meat-tree wake retries when the world state needs a fresh sync.
Trade offers now show stack quantities correctly, make offered items disappear from your remaining inventory count, support right-click Add 1, 5, 10, X, or All, and use a cleaner completion flash.
The Washing Machine deposit list now uses the same right-click quantity menu and no longer shows the extra instruction paragraph.
The SlimeCoin Shop has a larger, cleaner preview area, clearer tabs, bigger icons and text, centered item text, and safer cosmetic preview swapping.

title: Stability and Reliability
entries: Background progress, reconnects, mobile wake, and return-from-away flows are more consistent.
Combat and boss sessions are steadier when the game is unfocused or recovering from a pause.
Exchange posting and washer actions handle stale item states more cleanly.
Backend reward accounting cleanup reduces false alarms without loosening hard protections.

title: Reliability Patch
entries: Background gathering and return-from-away handling are more reliable across long idle sessions, reconnects, and server-timed events.
This is a stability-focused update with no balance changes.

title: Teleport Animation and Fixes
entries: Companion Teleport now uses the proper Home Teleport-style cast animation for map clicks, cursor teleports, saved teleport presets, and friend teleports.
Teleporting from the full map now closes the map first, so the teleport animation is visible.
Regular daily caves now remember mounted horse state correctly, matching the existing cave and Pet Garden restore behavior.
Stormwood-tier treasure logs now sort into the Logs bank tab instead of Loot/default.
Item broadcasts now keep Boss Damage and other current potential stat lines visible.

title: Teleport Favorites, Boat Fuel and Fixes
entries: Teleport favorites are here: right-click the map Teleport arm button to save up to 5 local spots, then use them from the Teleports section while you have Teleport charges.
Teleport favorite icons are optional from that same right-click menu, and clicking a shown saved spot teleports there.
Teleport targeting can now be disarmed by clicking the arm button again, and closing the map clears targeting.
Boat boost fuel now scales by boat tier, giving higher-tier boats a longer boost meter while boost speed stays the same.
Bonfire seafood recipes now recognize newer cooked fish like Cooked Oarfish, Cooked Gulper Eel, and Cooked Anglerking in the seafood tier order.
Banks and other nearby objects stay clickable when zoomed far out on the map.
Long background or AFK graphics recovery is safer, with a restore overlay instead of leaving players on a white screen.
Session takeover reloads are safer and should no longer bounce you into the duplicate-tab warning after taking control.
Mobile long-press now opens the normal right-click action flow.

title: Farm Tracker Polish
entries: Farm tracker Pet Slime labels now render as readable tags above the slime instead of a tiny warped box.
Tracker color picking now stays contextual to the crop, bush, or tree tracker you right-clicked, and tracker slime colors no longer bleed between trackers.
Farm tracker right-click menus now stay on-screen when the tracker is near the bottom or edge of the window.

title: Friend Teleport and Farming QoL
entries: Friend Teleport can now find an online friend who is far away, instead of only working when their avatar is already nearby.
Farm tracker Pet Slime labels are now a simple Crop, Bush, or Tree toggle, with no browser pop-up naming box.
Quick Plant menus now grey out plant choices you cannot use yet because of level or seed coin cost.

title: Farm Tracker Slots
entries: Farm trackers can now keep one crop, one bush, and one tree tracker active at the same time.
Tracking another patch of the same type replaces that slot while keeping that slot's position, slime color, and optional custom name.
Right-click a tracker to rename it, close it, pick its Pet Slime color, or swap between Bar, Compact, Ring, Clock, and Pet Slime modes.

title: Island Meteor Reliability
entries: Island Meteors are now separated from regular Meteors, with Coal Miner rough hints, click-to-reveal map markers, and one shared 1,500 HP pool.
Deep Sea Shoals stay lightweight and discoverable through Coal Miner or by sailing near the live shoal.
Farm trackers can now track multiple patches at once, with Bar, Compact, Progress Ring, Countdown Clock, and per-tracker Pet Slime colors.
Bank Favorites now appear in both the bank header and the preset sidebar.

title: Island Meteor Prep
entries: Prepared client-side Island Meteor signal and visibility handling. The full reliable Island Meteor split is in v1347.

title: Shoal Signal Prep
entries: Improved Deep Sea Shoal rough signals and click-to-reveal behavior while Island Meteors were still under testing.

title: Shoals, Cape Perks & QoL
entries: Added Deep Sea Shoals, Farming Cape patch binding, Mining Cape meteor shard doubling, and improved Friend Teleport.
Cooking, farm tracking, bank/map UI, pet pouches, and several visual/animation fixes were cleaned up in this batch.
Initial Island Meteor testing started here; the reliable independent Island Meteor event is in v1347.

title: Farming QoL and Mining Mastery Rewards
entries: Farming patch QoL now includes Quick Plant for crops, trees, and berry bushes with a toggle and context-menu selection, plus right-click setup from both the crop selector and patch menus.

You can now track any farm patch directly from the patch menu; the tracker HUD mirrors XP/buff timer style and shows the patch icon, timer, and a "Done" green state, with a subtle 3-ding completion chime.
Right-click a growing crop to remove it and clear the patch quickly.
You can now scroll the Quick Plant menu to choose a seed without it closing.
The new Mining-1000 reward flow is now live with Mining Cape, Crystal Aura, and Meteor Mine emote support.
New world helpers and Coal Miner placement are available in the game world and editor.
Coal Miner now uses a baked portrait icon in the icon export cache (same format as Farmer Sprout), so mined dialogues use cached art instead of a runtime-only render.

title: Farming, Friends & QoL Fixes
entries: Farming crop sell values are now documented correctly: the high-tier crop band was lowered in the store, with Sugar Plum selling for 1,330c and Golden Sugarplum selling for 3,200c.
Golden Sugarplum also uses the intended 2-hour grow time, 0c seed cost, fixed 100 compost charge cost, 120,000 XP, and 12-20 harvest yield.
Cleaned up stale crop metadata so farming config, generated displays, and store sell values use the same numbers instead of showing old planning values.
Bank favorites now only appear in bank right-click menus, instead of being added to normal inventory item menus outside the bank.

Friend Teleport now appears for online friends in both Friends views. The teleport hotkey jumps to your current cursor target, or arms a crosshair map target when the big map is open; the map title updates while armed, and the Arm Teleport button only appears when you have charges.
If your account is already active somewhere else, the stopped-session screen now includes a safe Kick Other Sessions & Reload option that takes control and reloads fresh cloud progress.
Throwing logs into Frost Yeti braziers is much lighter now, reducing the hitch from brazier ember effects.

title: Cooking, Bank & Ocean QoL
entries: The bonfire cooking panel now lets you pick flexible ingredients, cook from your full available supply, and safely batch recipes without losing ingredients when inventory space is tight.
New cooking options are live, including Farmer Pie, Grand Farmer Pie, and legendary Jelly recipes, with recipe XP now giving fair credit for stronger ingredients.
Bank favorites now sit on the side below preset B. Right-click items in either the bank item grid or the inventory area while banking to add or remove favorites, then tap the star to filter them.
Audio settings now split sound effects into Player Combat, Boss Combat, Rare Foraging, and Legendary Drops, so rare foraging chimes and big legendary fanfares can be controlled separately.
Lucky Duck and Ocean Friend click targets are easier to select, right-click menus include their actions, and the Slime Shop preview now shows the same 2D slime icon style used on the minimap.

Trading Post buy-offer fills now cap the quantity picker to the matching items you actually own, and login/save handling is more careful when a cloud load fails instead of treating it like a fresh account.

title: DPS Calculator
entries: New DPS Calculator — open it from the small “DPS” button on your equipment panel to see your expected damage per second, max hit, accuracy, attack speed and time-to-kill against any monster, all matching the in-game combat numbers.

It reads your live worn gear and lets you experiment freely: re-roll potential lines, swap combat style, set tempo stacks, toggle prayers, buffs, potions and pet perks, pick any target, and compare two loadouts side by side — nothing you do in it ever touches your real save or gear.
Planted farm patches now use much lighter models, so having crops in view is easier on performance.

title: Performance & Crafting Refresh
entries: Big performance pass for smoother gameplay — frame rate is noticeably better while riding, in crowds, and on high render-distance or max settings, with the worst hitching from nearby slimes and hover/minimap stutter gone.
The Crafting Table has a fresh look that matches the bank, and arrow recipes now show real arrow icons with their wood and ore ingredients fully readable.

Cooking now uses your cheapest qualifying ingredient instead of your most valuable one, so batch-cooking no longer quietly burns high-tier crops like Sugar Plums, and recipe requirements now show exactly what is needed (for example 'Fruit (Stormberry+)').
Giant Rat, Bee, and Swamp Frog now have their own Slimetrics hiscore boards and show up in the Recent Boss Kills feed.
The in-game Guide now includes the three newest bosses and lists the 1-in-1000 Paintbrush cosmetic that every boss can drop.
Party XP sharing is now combat-only, so gathering keeps all of its own XP, and shared XP is capped so it can no longer power-level a low character dozens of levels from a single boss kill.

title: Combat & Woodcutting Fixes
entries: Woodcutting frenzies that land on planted tree patches can now always be collected — clicking any part of the patch picks up the frenzy instead of it sometimes feeling dead.
Clicking to attack a moving or out-of-range enemy now walks you to it and swings once you arrive, instead of standing still and swinging at where it used to be.
Fixed the Swamp Frog's punch attack so it plays its proper animation.

title: New Bosses & Farming Mastery
entries: Three new bosses are here: Giant Rat, Bee, and Swamp Frog, with new arenas, drops, guide entries, kill-count tracking, and leaderboard support.
New boss loot: Rat Cheese, Honeycomb, Frog Legs, Rat/Bee/Frog paintbrushes, new equipment rolls, and rare Bee/Frog high-tier equipment drops.
Three new boss scroll perks can now be crafted: Rat Scroll for Def Shred, Flower Scroll for Pet Call, and Swamp Scroll for Giga Mode.
Farming 1000 now has a Farmer Sprout mastery reward flow with the best-in-slot Farming Cape, Leaf Aura, Golden Sugarplums, and the Sugar Plum Master cape emote.
The Farming Cape emote can be played from the cape menu and is visible to nearby players.
Golden Sugarplums are unlocked at Farming 1000 as a premium crop with 120,000 XP, 12-20 yield, and 8,000 coin sell value.
Swamp Frog can rarely drop the Log Basket, which stores up to 100 high-level treasure logs while chopping.
Stormwood, Leviathan Kelp, Gooey Wood, Prismwood, and Infinity Wood now have faster chop cycles and doubled sell values.
Frog's final 5-fly swarm now instakills if you miss the correct prayer, and its tongue punch damage is synced to the punch animation.
Settings now has a Release Media Keys toggle so game music stops grabbing keyboard play/pause controls while sound effects keep playing.
Fixed tab-return reconnects blanking other players, fixed inventory icons waiting for an item change, and fixed new bosses giving SlimeCoins from attack rolls.

title: Bug Fix Round
entries: Flame Aura and Sparkle Aura now show up in the Slime Shop preview, so you can see them on your slime before you buy.
Sailing tasks that ask for cedar on a volcanic island now say "Burnt Logs" and point you at the burnt trees that actually drop them, instead of sending you hunting for a cedar tree that isn't there.
Mining at a safespot while the tab is in the background no longer auto-attacks an enemy that can't actually reach you, so it stops cancelling your mining.
The Adventure Book Forge stats now read "Enhance coins spent" and "Washer coins spent" instead of showing a leftover bit of code.

title: Auto-Wash & Pickup Fixes
entries: The Washing Machine can now auto-roll for you: pick the potential line you want and a minimum value, then it keeps washing on its own and stops the moment it rolls a match.
Dropped items sitting behind or under a tree can now be picked up by clicking them, instead of doing nothing and canceling your gathering.
When you tab back into the game, other players no longer disappear for a second before popping back in.

title: Crafting Fixes & Skele Speedup
entries: Spinning, cooking, scribing, enchanting, and buying in huge quantities no longer freezes or crashes the game, so giant stacks process instantly.
Scribing, enchanting, spellstone crafting, and bonfire cooking now have a small chance to award SlimeCoins, while the spin wheel's SlimeCoin rate has been reduced.
The Skele boss phase transitions are much faster now, so there is far less waiting between phases.

title: Security & Server Upgrades
entries: The SlimeCoin economy has been rebuilt to run server-side end to end, so shop purchases feel fast while staying more reliable.
A broad security pass now protects more of the economy, saves, combat, XP, item drops, PvP, the Dojo, and leaderboards.
Server, networking, save-system, database, and backend upgrades make the game sturdier as more players pile in.
You should also feel the recent improvements: more reliable SlimeCoin purchases, fixed player-to-player trades, smoother performance, and many bug fixes.

title: Performance & Stability
entries: Premium animated skins stay crisp now and no longer flicker or briefly break when other players move out of view.
Smoother performance and lower memory use over long play sessions, repeated boss fights, and back-to-back cave runs.

title: Messages Always Reach
entries: Direct messages and game invites now reach an online friend no matter where they are in the game, so a message always goes through when your friend is online.

title: Friends List Fixes
entries: Online friends in your friends list show their Game and Party invite buttons, so you can invite them straight to a mini-game or your party.
After choosing a chat channel from the dropdown, pressing Enter now drops your cursor into the chat box so you can start typing right away.

title: Friend Hub Redesign
entries: The Friends panel has a fresh look with Friends and Ignored tabs. Add a friend or someone to ignore by typing their name right in the panel, with no browser popup, and remove anyone from either list.
Reply to a direct message faster: right-click a friend's message and pick Reply, or press Tab to reply to the most recent one.
Open the Game Hub from your friends list, and use the new Back to Friends button to return to it.

title: Friend Presence & Inspect Polish
entries: Your friends list now shows who's online more reliably, with clearer status when an invite can't reach a friend right now.
Equipment comparison tooltips now show a small equals sign when Attack or Defense matches your currently equipped item.

title: Social Polish & Pet Garden Header
entries: Clan chat messages now show a small Clan badge, and recent clan chat can replay correctly after reconnecting without showing another clan's messages.
Friend direct messages now appear in chat as clean From/To friend lines instead of generic status updates.
Friends are easier to manage: the Friends channel opens the picker reliably, the Friend Hub refreshes fresh, and friend rows now use clear Msg, Game, Party, and Remove buttons.
Removing a friend, declining a request, or canceling a sent request now uses an in-game confirmation.
The Friend Hub button now flashes and shows a count badge when you have pending friend requests.
The Pet Garden panel has a bigger draggable header. The pet selector sits under it, and the slimelet color/shape customizer opens in the top empty space above the portrait.

title: Friends, Clan Chat & Party XP
entries: New friends list and ignore list, tied to your account. Right-click a player to add or ignore, or add a friend by name from the Friends panel. Mutual requests auto-accept.
Clan chat works now and reaches only your clan.
The chat dropdown has a new Friends option: see your friends and send private messages, with replies shown right in chat.
Invite a friend straight into a game from your friends list, or open the Game Hub from there.
Party XP sharing: party members in the same area share experience. You keep most of yours, the rest is split nearby, and you never lose XP when you are on your own.

Fixes: rare drops land in the open near you instead of inside hills or bushes and right-click Pick up walks you over to grab it, resizing a popped-out panel like Emotes is smooth, meteors no longer get stuck after locking your phone mid-mine on mobile, and Roots are easier to click.

title: Respawn Hotfix
entries: Fixed deaths where the ghost could fade out before you clicked Respawn in boss, dungeon, or PvP-style death states.
Refreshed the Garden Helper changelog wording so it describes the final release cleanly instead of an internal tuning pass.

title: Garden Helper, Respawn Choice & Crafting Feedback
entries: New Garden Helper quest: Farmer Sprout now walks you through the Pet Garden with dialogue, quest markers, highlighted buttons, and rewards. If you already have a slimelet, he recognizes it and skips the forced hatch.

Garden onboarding is smoother: the first slimelet is free, Sprout gives 100 Tree Meat, Meat Trees unlock at Woodcutting 30 with level-scaled XP, and Sprout gives you a small starter scroll to try the system.
Farmer Sprout has a proper baked portrait and a cleaner dialogue panel with the portrait on the left and edge-to-edge dialogue controls.
The shared pouch step now highlights Collect and lets you press it even if the pouch is empty, so you learn the action without waiting for +1 item.
Crafting now shows a lightweight result toast inside the crafting UI with the crafted item icon and rarity, without closing your inventory.
Deaths now wait for you to click Respawn instead of instantly snapping you back.

Fixes and polish: party boss entry crashes are fixed, stacked mobs can be chosen from the right-click menu, mailbox sale payouts can show which items sold, buff rings were polished, prayer tooltips no longer stick, Rat Slimes are back near the early mainland path, and Garden music was updated.

title: Combat & Drop Fixes
entries: Minimized AFK combat no longer wanders off to attack peaceful animals like cats, and giant slimes can no longer path through safespots while your game tab is in the background.
Rare fruit drops such as Sun Peach now show a clean center-screen popup instead of a floating label that could streak across the screen and look like a camera jerk at certain camera angles.

title: HUD Ring Polish
entries: The buff and boost timer ring now matches the XP tracker ring. It is the same size by default and drains clockwise the same way.
In the compact ring, the number now sits centered directly under the skill icon, with the percent sign tucked neatly beside it.

title: Pet Garden Overhaul, HUD Customization & New Fruits
entries: The Pet Garden is now a shared space. You'll meet other players there, see their slimelets wander in and out through a new cave entrance, and you can click one to pet it.

Plant in the garden: Tree and Bush patches are real farm patches now, and the new Magic Patch takes Magic Seeds. Each seed keeps your pets fed (full hunger) for 10 hours, so they stay effective. The crew still needs Tree Meat in the trough to gather.
The garden now has its own Bank Chest, you can roam much farther out into the meadow, and you keep your horse after boss, dungeon, and PvP runs instead of losing it.
The slimelet collection popup now shows item icons in a cleaner layout.

Make the HUD yours: drag any HUD piece to move it, hold Alt and drag to resize it, and Shift plus right-click to reset it to default position and size. Works on the HP bar, boss bar, buff bar, XP tracker, and the leave banner.
The buff/boost bar has a fresh look with round timer rings. Right-click a buff to change its ring style, color, font, or size.

The XP tracker has a new 'Compact Ring (Level)' mode and is colored by skill, the buff bar and XP tracker swapped default spots, there's a new 'Hide Game Hub Button' setting, and 'Reset UI to Default' now snaps everything back instantly.
Five new legendary fruits drop from the high-tier berry bushes: Storm Guava, Leviathan Mango, Sludge Papaya, Prism Kiwi, and Infinity Plum. Each has a new bonfire dish to cook.
Binding Spellstones is much cheaper: 1 Meteor Shard plus 1 catalyst now makes 10 Spellstones.
Chat polish: clearing chat sticks after reconnecting, you can right-click to copy selected chat text, and right-click a chat box to paste.

title: Meteor Frenzy Glow-Up, PvP Polish & Fixes
entries: Meteor frenzy is a spectacle now: the meteor glows molten-orange while it lasts, you earn 10x XP, and you scoop up a burst of bonus Meteor Shards.
Break down Meteor Cores: right-click a Meteor Core to turn each one into 10 Meteor Shards for binding Spellstones.
PvP in the Crucible now attacks at a smooth, continuous speed that matches your normal PvM rhythm - the old start-stop stutter is gone, and damage is unchanged.
On mobile, reconnecting after your phone has been locked for a while now brings other players back onto the map and minimap right away, instead of showing an empty world while you appear online.
Spellstones now show their proper color names everywhere in the Spellstone Pool menu and its status messages.
Shoreline foam now lines up cleanly with the water when viewed from a distance, and Torches are properly lit and shaded to match the world.

title: World Streaming, Slime Restyle & Smoother Play
entries: New World Streaming 'Edge Reveal' (Settings - Camera): the world becomes a clean slab that slides with you, with sky beyond the edge - on by default (Circle) and sized to show your whole render-distance area.
Restyle your slimelets: recolor and reshape any slimelet right from the Pet Garden. Pets also get hungry and unhappy far more slowly, and the Pet Treat costs less.
Smoother camera panning and lighter distant water, plus scroll icons are now tinted by family and tier so they are easy to tell apart.
New 'Hide Combat Style Panel' setting, ground items are bigger and sit on the ground, Spellstones now have clear color names, and Home Teleport works while you are gathering.
Home Teleport and dropped items now save right away - teleporting or dropping something and quickly refreshing no longer sends you back to the old spot or returns the item.
Fixed false 'logged in from another browser' and 'Reload Required - progress continued while asleep' warnings that could interrupt normal single-device and AFK play.
PvP in the Crucible feels tighter - your hit splat appears the instant you swing, you attack at your normal speed, and damage is unchanged; also fixed PvP damage on guest accounts.
Fixes: a rare graphics console error is gone, and these patch notes use a clearer, larger font.

title: Minimized Combat, UI Refresh & Fixes
entries: Combat now keeps going while the game is minimized — your slime keeps fighting and chains kills as monsters respawn, for all styles, with sounds. It runs at normal speed with no bonus XP, for up to 15 minutes after your last input.
Monsters now make a soft little grunt when they die.
Fixed some monsters (like raccoons and wolves) being counted twice — which doubled their loot drops and the damage they dealt you. Also fixed a double-swing when tabbing back in.
Farm bushes now grow into the right plant — berry and Voidberry bushes were showing wrong or invisible models.
The Bank and General Store have a clean new edge-to-edge look. The Farm and Compost windows no longer overflow the screen.
World Map: picking a resource found in the PvP cave now pings the cave entrance.
Nights are smoother and warmer — less red with a gentler glow. Torches, campfires and bonfires now share one improved flame.
The day/night cycle is optimized for no stutter, and its extra night detail (stars, clouds and the sunset glow) scales to your graphics quality.
The Ghost Slime spot is back, in the world and on the map.
Security: PvP damage is capped by your real, server-verified level to stop level-spoof one-shots, bounties are set by the server, and being kicked no longer locks you out.

title: Day/Night Cycle, Torches, Iceberg & Bush Patches
entries: New Day/Night Cycle: the overworld sky now moves through a full day on your local clock — sunrise and sunset color shifts, the sun arcing and setting behind the hills, a moon, twinkling stars and drifting clouds, with nights gently dimming the world. On by default; toggle it in Camera settings (Day/Night Cycle).
Torches you can place light up at night: ambient torches around the world now glow and flicker after dark for cozier night-time exploring.
New floating Iceberg: walk out onto the drifting ice floe off the coast — it gently rocks and tips toward whichever side everyone crowds onto.

New Bush Patches for farming: plant a berry bush, watch it grow, and pick berries for Foraging XP plus a first-pick Farming bonus — 16 tiers from Berry Bush up to Astral Berry Bush. Right-click Remove salvages the remaining berries (with a chance at an Apple).

Combat: attack-speed (Tempo) retuned — deep Tempo lines keep adding real attack speed instead of trailing off, while early lines still hit hardest. Glass-cannon builds reach about 6 attacks/sec (~6.7 with Comet).

Fixes: the Acorn Cap cosmetic's shading is corrected, procgen-island fishing pins on the World Map now line up with the real world, and in Classic Layout the night dimming no longer darkens your sidebar/minimap/HUD.
Security & anti-cheat: closed an item-duplication hole and hardened PvP loot and hit detection so cheaters can't dupe or launder items or spoof teleport/dodge hits.

title: Slimetrics Stats Site Fixes
entries: Slimetrics (the stats site) 'Recent Highlights' feed is fixed — it had been stuck for a few days and stopped showing new level-ups. They'll start showing again as players hit level milestones.
Slimetrics is faster and lighter: player profiles, leaderboards and the Server page do a lot less work to load, and pages you've already opened come back instantly.
No in-game changes this update — it's all stats-site improvements.

title: EXP Tracker Rebuilt & Fixes
entries: EXP Tracker rebuilt: a proper bar again — current level on the left, next level on the right, filling as you train (plus a Compact Ring view). XP/hour and 'actions to level' are now accurate — no more giant fake numbers after logging in or alt-tabbing. Renamed 'EXP Tracker (Auto)' in Settings.
Pet Treats now work — feeding one to a slimelet was broken (the button greyed out even with treats in your bag). They also show their proper candy icon now.
AFK timers: Hemp, Flax and all the Lvl 600+ berries now use the full 6-hour gather timer like every other berry; the Lvl 600+ trees get a new 1-hour timer (slow chop, 12 min was too short).
World Map: fishing spots show the right level + a fish icon, every berry has its own icon, and Farm patches now live under a new Farming section.
New 'What's New' button on the login screen — patch notes in-game (this very thing!).
Pet Garden now respects your graphics settings (Potato Mode trims the decorations), runs smoother, and a stale browser tab can no longer wipe your pouch + Pet Treats.
Fixes: removing a farmed tree no longer walks you to spawn; remote players load smoother; fixed rare overlapping trees/rocks on some far islands; new Hide Hover Ring setting.

title: Pet Garden Rework & Polish
entries: Pet Garden "Miscellania" rework: one shared pouch for the whole crew, slimelet levels now boost gather RATE (not bag size), a feeding trough fueled by Tree Meat, and hunger/happiness that actually affects how much they gather.
New Pet Treat (SlimeCoin shop): permanently boosts a slimelet's gather rate and lets well-fed pets look after themselves.
Brand-new Pet Garden scene: fresh terrain, a real river + waterfall, a bouncy diving board, a climbable summit, butterflies and more.
Exclusive fishing spots: some spots now only yield one species — and you can see the fish swimming as a shadow in the water.

EXP Tracker overhaul: cleaner bar that fills toward your next level with the current/next level on each end, plus a Compact Ring view. XP/hour and "actions to level" are now accurate (no more giant fake numbers after logging in or alt-tabbing).
World Map resource list: fishing spots show the right level + a fitting icon, berries got their own icons, and farm patches now live under a Farming section.
New setting: Hide Hover Ring. Redesigned scrolls and staves/wands, two-tone wood log icons, and remade fruit + crop models.

title: Endgame Trees & Skill Tracker
entries: New level 600–1000 woodcutting trees in the PvP Crucible: a high-risk, high-reward treasure-log haul loop.
New top-tier woodcutting frenzy: Enchanted Relic at level 600.
New Skill Tracker HUD chip — right-click a skill to track your progress (now reworked further in v1133).
Fixed a pet garden double-claim issue when a pet's cache was full.

title: World Overhaul & Keybinds
entries: World model overhaul: trees, bushes, rocks and props remodeled across the map.
New Keybinds tab — rebind movement, map, guide, quick-inventory and quick-worn.
New Camera settings tab.
Chat history now replays recent messages when you join, so you don't miss the room.
Mobile tap-through fixes for menus and game controls.
